# HelpChatbot — RAG-Based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem Statement

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that allows customers to ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system will use a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDF documents are ingested, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres (with pgvector). At query time, relevant chunks are retrieved via vector similarity search and fed to an LLM to generate answers with source attribution.

## Technology Selection

Based on the .NET AI technology decision tree:

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response with RAG context (no agentic loops needed) |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Widely available; swap to Azure OpenAI later via DI |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** | Postgres-native vector search; reuses existing DB infrastructure |
| Document ingestion | **Custom ingestion service** using `PdfPig` for PDF parsing + manual chunking | Full control over chunk boundaries and metadata |
| Embedding generation | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Same abstraction layer as chat; provider-swappable |
| Web UI | **Blazor Server (Interactive)** | Real-time streaming responses; no separate API layer needed |
| Data storage | **EF Core + Npgsql** on Postgres | Chat history, user sessions |
| PDF source | **File system folder** (`Docs/`) | Simple; documents dropped into folder and ingested on demand |

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Blazor Server UI (Chat Component)                 │
└────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
┌────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│  ChatService                                       │
│  - Accepts user query                              │
│  - Calls EmbeddingGenerator to embed query         │
│  - Searches vector store for relevant chunks       │
│  - Builds prompt with retrieved context            │
│  - Streams LLM response via IChatClient            │
└────────────────┬──────────────┬────────────────────┘
                 │              │
    ┌────────────▼──┐   ┌──────▼─────────────────┐
    │  Postgres     │   │  OpenAI / Azure OpenAI  │
    │  - pgvector   │   │  (IChatClient +         │
    │  - chat hist  │   │   IEmbeddingGenerator)  │
    └───────────────┘   └─────────────────────────┘

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  IngestionService (background / on-demand)         │
│  - Reads PDFs from Docs/ folder                    │
│  - Parses with PdfPig                              │
│  - Chunks text (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)      │
│  - Generates embeddings                            │
│  - Upserts into pgvector                           │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup and dependencies
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, pgvector Npgsql integration, UglyToad.PdfPig, Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Add Blazor Server interactive rendering to the project

### 2. Configuration and DI
- Create `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string (placeholder) and AI model settings
- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, EF Core DbContext, vector store, and custom services via DI
- Use `IOptions<T>` for AI config (model name, temperature, max tokens)

### 3. Database layer
- Create EF Core DbContext with entities: `ChatSession`, `ChatMessage`, `DocumentChunk`
- `DocumentChunk` holds: Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding (vector)
- Create initial EF migration for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text into ~500-token segments with overlap using paragraph boundaries
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upserts chunks into Postgres vector store
- Exposed as a background service that runs on startup + an admin endpoint to re-trigger

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` accepts a user message and session ID
- Embeds the query, searches pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min score threshold)
- Constructs a system prompt with retrieved context and source attribution instructions
- Streams the LLM response back using `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync`
- Saves messages to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- Create a chat page component (`Chat.razor`) with:
  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source references shown below answers
- Basic layout with navigation

### 7. Guardrails and production readiness
- Set temperature=0 for factual responses
- Pin model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Add retry logic for LLM calls
- Set `MaxOutputTokens` limit
- Relevance threshold on vector search (min score 0.75)
- Graceful fallback when no relevant chunks found
- API key loaded from environment variable / user-secrets (not committed)

## Notes

- The project targets **.NET 10** (already configured in .csproj)
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single-turn RAG Q&A, not an agentic workflow
- pgvector extension must be enabled in the target Postgres database (`CREATE EXTENSION vector`)

# HelpChatbot — RAG-Based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem Statement

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that allows customers to ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system will use a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDF documents are ingested, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres (with pgvector). At query time, relevant chunks are retrieved via vector similarity search and fed to an LLM to generate answers with source attribution.

## Technology Selection

Based on the .NET AI technology decision tree:

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response with RAG context (no agentic loops needed) |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Widely available; swap to Azure OpenAI later via DI |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** | Postgres-native vector search; reuses existing DB infrastructure |
| Document ingestion | **Custom ingestion service** using `PdfPig` for PDF parsing + manual chunking | Full control over chunk boundaries and metadata |
| Embedding generation | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Same abstraction layer as chat; provider-swappable |
| Web UI | **Blazor Server (Interactive)** | Real-time streaming responses; no separate API layer needed |
| Data storage | **EF Core + Npgsql** on Postgres | Chat history, user sessions |
| PDF source | **File system folder** (`Docs/`) | Simple; documents dropped into folder and ingested on demand |

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Blazor Server UI (Chat Component)                 │
└────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
┌────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│  ChatService                                       │
│  - Accepts user query                              │
│  - Calls EmbeddingGenerator to embed query         │
│  - Searches vector store for relevant chunks       │
│  - Builds prompt with retrieved context            │
│  - Streams LLM response via IChatClient            │
└────────────────┬──────────────┬────────────────────┘
                 │              │
    ┌────────────▼──┐   ┌──────▼─────────────────┐
    │  Postgres     │   │  OpenAI / Azure OpenAI  │
    │  - pgvector   │   │  (IChatClient +         │
    │  - chat hist  │   │   IEmbeddingGenerator)  │
    └───────────────┘   └─────────────────────────┘

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  IngestionService (background / on-demand)         │
│  - Reads PDFs from Docs/ folder                    │
│  - Parses with PdfPig                              │
│  - Chunks text (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)      │
│  - Generates embeddings                            │
│  - Upserts into pgvector                           │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup and dependencies
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, pgvector Npgsql integration, UglyToad.PdfPig, Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Add Blazor Server interactive rendering to the project

### 2. Configuration and DI
- Create `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string (placeholder) and AI model settings
- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, EF Core DbContext, vector store, and custom services via DI
- Use `IOptions<T>` for AI config (model name, temperature, max tokens)

### 3. Database layer
- Create EF Core DbContext with entities: `ChatSession`, `ChatMessage`, `DocumentChunk`
- `DocumentChunk` holds: Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding (vector)
- Create initial EF migration for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text into ~500-token segments with overlap using paragraph boundaries
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upserts chunks into Postgres vector store
- Exposed as a background service that runs on startup + an admin endpoint to re-trigger

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` accepts a user message and session ID
- Embeds the query, searches pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min score threshold)
- Constructs a system prompt with retrieved context and source attribution instructions
- Streams the LLM response back using `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync`
- Saves messages to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- Create a chat page component (`Chat.razor`) with:
  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source references shown below answers
- Basic layout with navigation

### 7. Guardrails and production readiness
- Set temperature=0 for factual responses
- Pin model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Add retry logic for LLM calls
- Set `MaxOutputTokens` limit
- Relevance threshold on vector search (min score 0.75)
- Graceful fallback when no relevant chunks found
- API key loaded from environment variable / user-secrets (not committed)

## Notes

- The project targets **.NET 10** (already configured in .csproj)
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single-turn RAG Q&A, not an agentic workflow
- pgvector extension must be enabled in the target Postgres database (`CREATE EXTENSION vector`)
- For local dev, user can run Postgres via Docker
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`

# HelpChatbot — RAG-Based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem Statement

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that allows customers to ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. The system will use a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDF documents are ingested, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres (with pgvector). At query time, relevant chunks are retrieved via vector similarity search and fed to an LLM to generate answers with source attribution.

## Technology Selection

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response with RAG context (no agentic loops needed) |
| LLM provider | **OpenAI** (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Widely available; swap to Azure OpenAI later via DI |
| Vector store | **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** | Postgres-native vector search; reuses existing DB infrastructure |
| Document ingestion | **Custom ingestion service** using `PdfPig` for PDF parsing + manual chunking | Full control over chunk boundaries and metadata |
| Embedding generation | **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Same abstraction layer as chat; provider-swappable |
| Web UI | **Blazor Server (Interactive)** | Real-time streaming responses; no separate API layer needed |
| Data storage | **EF Core + Npgsql** on Postgres | Chat history, user sessions |
| PDF source | **File system folder** (`Docs/`) | Simple; documents dropped into folder and ingested on demand |

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Blazor Server UI (Chat Component)                 │
└────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘
                 │
┌────────────────▼───────────────────────────────────┐
│  ChatService                                       │
│  - Accepts user query                              │
│  - Calls EmbeddingGenerator to embed query         │
│  - Searches vector store for relevant chunks       │
│  - Builds prompt with retrieved context            │
│  - Streams LLM response via IChatClient            │
└────────────────┬──────────────┬────────────────────┘
                 │              │
    ┌────────────▼──┐   ┌──────▼─────────────────┐
    │  Postgres     │   │  OpenAI / Azure OpenAI  │
    │  - pgvector   │   │  (IChatClient +         │
    │  - chat hist  │   │   IEmbeddingGenerator)  │
    └───────────────┘   └─────────────────────────┘

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  IngestionService (background / on-demand)         │
│  - Reads PDFs from Docs/ folder                    │
│  - Parses with PdfPig                              │
│  - Chunks text (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)      │
│  - Generates embeddings                            │
│  - Upserts into pgvector                           │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup and dependencies
- Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.Extensions.AI, OpenAI, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions, Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL, pgvector Npgsql integration, UglyToad.PdfPig, Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Add Blazor Server interactive rendering to the project

### 2. Configuration and DI
- Create `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string (placeholder) and AI model settings
- Register `IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`, EF Core DbContext, vector store, and custom services via DI
- Use `IOptions<T>` for AI config (model name, temperature, max tokens)

### 3. Database layer
- Create EF Core DbContext with entities: `ChatSession`, `ChatMessage`, `DocumentChunk`
- `DocumentChunk` holds: Id, DocumentName, ChunkIndex, Text, Embedding (vector)
- Create initial EF migration for Postgres + pgvector extension

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text into ~500-token segments with overlap using paragraph boundaries
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Upserts chunks into Postgres vector store
- Exposed as a background service that runs on startup + an admin endpoint to re-trigger

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService` accepts a user message and session ID
- Embeds the query, searches pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min score threshold)
- Constructs a system prompt with retrieved context and source attribution instructions
- Streams the LLM response back using `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync`
- Saves messages to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- Create a chat page component (`Chat.razor`) with:
  - Message list (user + assistant bubbles)
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source references shown below answers
- Basic layout with navigation

### 7. Guardrails and production readiness
- Set temperature=0 for factual responses
- Pin model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Add retry logic for LLM calls
- Set `MaxOutputTokens` limit
- Relevance threshold on vector search (min score 0.75)
- Graceful fallback when no relevant chunks found
- API key loaded from environment variable / user-secrets (not committed)

## Notes

- The project targets **.NET 10** (already configured in .csproj)
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single-turn RAG Q&A, not an agentic workflow
- pgvector extension must be enabled in the target Postgres database (`CREATE EXTENSION vector`)
- For local dev, user can run Postgres via Docker
